principle.name: Reuse Before Creating
principle.description: Always check existing styles, components, and tokens before writing new CSS or creating new components. The design system is intentionally comprehensive.
principle.rationale: Duplication creates maintenance burden and visual inconsistency. A single source of truth ensures the design language evolves cohesively.

example.good: Use existing with accent color via Tailwind token
example.bad: Create a new GoldButton component with hardcoded oklch(0.60 0.11 78)

principle.name: Token-First Styling
principle.description: All colors, spacing, and radii must use CSS variables or Tailwind tokens. Never hardcode OKLCH values in components.
principle.rationale: CSS variables enable theme switching (dark/light) and centralized design decisions.

principle.name: Strict Visual Consistency
principle.description: All pages and components must match visually. Typography, spacing, colors, and animations must follow the established hierarchy.
principle.rationale: Inconsistent UI erodes trust and creates a fragmented user experience.

example.good:
Use
with font-heading text-3xl tracking-widest uppercase
example.bad: Apply a custom h3 style with different font-family and letter-spacing

principle.name: Composition Over Inheritance
principle.description: Build complex UI by composing existing components rather than creating deeply customized variants.
principle.rationale: Composition produces simpler, more testable components with clearer interfaces.

principle.name: Mobile-First Responsive
principle.description: Design for mobile first, then enhance for tablet and desktop using Tailwind's responsive prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:).
principle.rationale: Mobile-first ensures core experience is solid and progressive enhancement adds richness.

convention.rule: Use kebab-case for CSS classes, camelCase for React props, and PascalCase for component names
Use BEM-style modifiers for component variants: component--variant
All components must have explicit TypeScript interfaces for props
Use React.forwardRef for all reusable components that may need DOM ref access

typography_rules.rule: H1 (48px): Brand name and hero titles only
Body (18px): Paragraph text with relaxed line-height (1.6)
All headings use font-heading (Playfair Display / Bodoni Moda / Cinzel)
Body text uses font-body (Inter for UI, Cormorant Garamond for content)

spacing_rules.rule: Use Tailwind spacing scale via var(--size-*): p-4, m-6, gap-8
Container max-width: 1400px with 2rem padding on sides

pitfall.description: Hardcoding colors instead of using CSS variables
pitfall.why_problematic: Breaks theme switching and creates visual inconsistency when values drift
pitfall.correct_approach: Use Tailwind classes mapped to CSS variables: text-foreground, bg-accent, border-border

pitfall.description: Creating new components for one-off styling needs
pitfall.why_problematic: Increases bundle size and maintenance burden without reuse benefit
pitfall.correct_approach: Use Tailwind class combinations or className prop extension on existing components

pitfall.why_problematic: Creates jarring user experience and breaks the luxury feel
pitfall.correct_approach: Use shared constants from src/lib/animations.ts: fadeSlideUp, staggerContainer, pageTransition

pitfall.why_problematic: Users with light theme see broken or unreadable UI
pitfall.correct_approach: Test all changes in both data-appearance="dark" and data-theme="light" contexts

category.name: before_starting
category.item: Survey existing components, tokens, and CSS variables for reuse opportunities
Confirm the request does not duplicate existing functionality
Identify which pages/sections will be affected

category.name: during_implementation
category.item: Use only established tokens (colors, spacing, typography)
Apply BEM naming for any new CSS modifiers
Write TypeScript interfaces for all props
Ensure both dark and light mode compatibility

category.name: before_completion
category.item: Verify visual consistency with existing pages
Test responsive behavior at all breakpoints
Check for hardcoded values that should use tokens
